pull_request.title: Remove testhelper, test SimpleArrayPlex directly
pull_request.body:
## Summary

- Remove the C++ `TestSimpleArrayHelper` pybind binding and the entire `cpp/modmesh/testhelper/` directory
- Remove the `USE_PYTEST_HELPER_BINDING` CMake option and all references in CI workflows
- Remove `testhelper` submodule registration from `module.cpp` and `core.py`
- Replace the old `test_SimpleArray_casting` (which required the C++ helper) with new pytest-only test cases in `SimpleArrayPlexTC`

Close #720

## Old vs new test coverage

The old `test_SimpleArray_casting` method (in `SimpleArrayBasicTC`) relied on `TestSimpleArrayHelper` C++ pybind bindings. All scenarios it covered are now tested directly using the `.typed`, `.plex`, and `ndarray` Python APIs:

| Old test scenario (`test_SimpleArray_casting`) | New test case (`SimpleArrayPlexTC`) |
|---|---|
| All plex instances share `_modmesh.SimpleArray` type regardless of dtype (`type(plex_int32) is type(plex_float64)`) | `test_SimpleArrayPlex_uniform_type` |
| Plex with matching dtype accepted by C++ function (`helper.test_load_arrayin32_from_arrayplex(plex_int32)`) | `test_SimpleArrayPlex_cast_to_typed` — verifies `.typed` produces the correct typed class and `ndarray.dtype` matches |
| Plex with wrong dtype rejected by C++ function (`helper.test_load_arrayin32_from_arrayplex(plex_uint64)` → `TypeError`) | `test_SimpleArrayPlex_cast_dtype_mismatch` — verifies constructing a typed array from a wrong-dtype plex's ndarray raises `RuntimeError: dtype mismatch` |
| C++ cast returns correct typed class (`helper.test_cast_to_arrayint32()` matches `SimpleArrayInt32`) | `test_SimpleArrayPlex_typed_from_typed_constructor` — verifies typed from `plex.typed` matches typed from direct constructor |
| *(not previously covered)* | `test_SimpleArrayPlex_typed_roundtrip` — verifies plex → typed → plex type chain |
| *(not previously covered)* | `test_SimpleArrayPlex_typed_preserves_data` — verifies data integrity through `.typed` cast |

## Files removed

- `cpp/modmesh/testhelper/CMakeLists.txt`
- `cpp/modmesh/testhelper/pymod/testbuffer_pymod.hpp`
- `cpp/modmesh/testhelper/pymod/testbuffer_pymod.cpp`
- `cpp/modmesh/testhelper/pymod/wrap_TestSimpleArrayHelper.cpp`

## Files modified

- `CMakeLists.txt` — remove `USE_PYTEST_HELPER_BINDING` option
- `cpp/modmesh/CMakeLists.txt` — remove testhelper subdirectory and file references
- `cpp/modmesh/python/module.cpp` — remove testhelper submodule registration
- `modmesh/core.py` — remove `list_of_testhelper` and references
- `tests/test_buffer.py` — remove old test, add 6 new direct test cases
- `.github/workflows/devbuild.yml` — remove `USE_PYTEST_HELPER_BINDING` flags, consolidate redundant ON/OFF build steps
- `.github/workflows/lint.yml` — merge duplicate pilot build steps into one
- `.github/workflows/nouse_install.yml` — remove `USE_PYTEST_HELPER_BINDING` flag

## Test plan

- [x] All 136 tests in `test_buffer.py` pass locally
- [ ] CI builds succeed without `USE_PYTEST_HELPER_BINDING`

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

-            str(type(arrayplex_int32)), "<class '_modmesh.SimpleArray'>")
-        self.assertEqual(

-        self.assertEqual(

-            helper.test_load_arrayin32_from_arrayplex(arrayplex_int32), True)
-
-        # int32 and uint64 are different types

-                TypeError,
-                r"incompatible function arguments"):
-            helper.test_load_arrayin32_from_arrayplex(arrayplex_uint64)

-        self.assertTrue(type(array_int32) is type(array_int32_2))

-            str(type(array_int32)), "<class '_modmesh.SimpleArrayInt32'>")
-        self.assertEqual(
-            str(type(array_int32_2)), "<class '_modmesh.SimpleArrayInt32'>")
-
     def test_SimpleArray_SimpleArrayPlex_type_switch(self):
         arrayplex_int32 = modmesh.SimpleArray((2, 3, 4), dtype="int32")
 
@@ -3499,6 +3444,75 @@ def test_SimpleArrayPlex_set_item_ellipse(self):
                 for k in range(4):
                     self.assertEqual(stride_arr[i, j, k], sarr[i, j, k])
 
+    def test_SimpleArrayPlex_uniform_type(self):
+        arrayplex_int32 = modmesh.SimpleArray((2, 3, 4), dtype="int32")
+        arrayplex_uint64 = modmesh.SimpleArray((2, 3, 4), dtype="uint64")
+        arrayplex_float64 = modmesh.SimpleArray((2, 3, 4), dtype="float64")
+
+        # all plex instances share the same Python type regardless of dtype
+        self.assertTrue(type(arrayplex_int32) is type(arrayplex_uint64))
+        self.assertTrue(type(arrayplex_uint64) is type(arrayplex_float64))
+        self.assertEqual(
+            str(type(arrayplex_int32)), "<class '_modmesh.SimpleArray'>")
+        self.assertEqual(
+            str(type(arrayplex_uint64)), "<class '_modmesh.SimpleArray'>")
+        self.assertEqual(
+            str(type(arrayplex_float64)), "<class '_modmesh.SimpleArray'>")
+
+    def test_SimpleArrayPlex_cast_to_typed(self):
+        dtype_typed_map = {
+            "int32": ("_modmesh.SimpleArrayInt32", np.int32),
+            "uint64": ("_modmesh.SimpleArrayUint64", np.uint64),
+            "float64": ("_modmesh.SimpleArrayFloat64", np.float64),
+        }
+        for dtype, (typed_name, np_dtype) in dtype_typed_map.items():
+            plex = modmesh.SimpleArray((2, 3, 4), dtype=dtype)
+            typed = plex.typed
+            self.assertEqual(str(type(typed)),
+                             "<class '{}'>".format(typed_name))
+            # verify ndarray dtype matches the expected numpy dtype
+            self.assertEqual(typed.ndarray.dtype, np_dtype)
+
+    def test_SimpleArrayPlex_cast_dtype_mismatch(self):
+        # constructing a typed array from a wrong-dtype plex must fail
+        plex_i32 = modmesh.SimpleArray((2, 3, 4), dtype="int32")
+        ndarr_i32 = np.array(plex_i32, copy=False)
+        with self.assertRaisesRegex(RuntimeError, r"dtype mismatch"):
+            modmesh.SimpleArrayFloat64(array=ndarr_i32)
+
+        plex_f64 = modmesh.SimpleArray((2, 3, 4), dtype="float64")
+        ndarr_f64 = np.array(plex_f64, copy=False)
+        with self.assertRaisesRegex(RuntimeError, r"dtype mismatch"):
+            modmesh.SimpleArrayInt32(array=ndarr_f64)
+
+    def test_SimpleArrayPlex_typed_roundtrip(self):
+        plex = modmesh.SimpleArray((2, 3, 4), dtype="int32")
+        typed = plex.typed
+        plex2 = typed.plex
+
+        self.assertEqual(str(type(plex)), "<class '_modmesh.SimpleArray'>")
+        self.assertEqual(
+            str(type(typed)), "<class '_modmesh.SimpleArrayInt32'>")
+        self.assertEqual(str(type(plex2)), "<class '_modmesh.SimpleArray'>")
+
+    def test_SimpleArrayPlex_typed_preserves_data(self):
+        plex = modmesh.SimpleArray((4,), dtype="float64", value=3.14)
+        typed = plex.typed
+        for i in range(4):
+            self.assertEqual(typed[i], 3.14)
+
+    def test_SimpleArrayPlex_typed_from_typed_constructor(self):
+        # typed array built from the constructor directly
+        array_int32 = modmesh.SimpleArrayInt32((2, 3, 4))
+        # typed array obtained via plex.typed
+        array_int32_2 = modmesh.SimpleArray(
+            (2, 3, 4), dtype="int32").typed
+        self.assertTrue(type(array_int32) is type(array_int32_2))
+        self.assertEqual(
+            str(type(array_int32)), "<class '_modmesh.SimpleArrayInt32'>")
+        self.assertEqual(
+            str(type(array_int32_2)), "<class '_modmesh.SimpleArrayInt32'>")
+
 
 class SimpleArrayPlexAlignmentTC(unittest.TestCase):

commit_summaries.message:
Remove testhelper infrastructure, test SimpleArrayPlex directly in pytest

Remove the C++ TestSimpleArrayHelper pybind binding and the
USE_PYTEST_HELPER_BINDING CMake option. The SimpleArrayPlex API (.typed,
.plex, ndarray) is now mature enough to test directly from Python without
a dedicated C++ test helper.

Close #720
